title: Tender Ethiopian Lentil Misir Wot
description: A traditional Ethiopian lentil stew, sweetened with condensed milk and spiced with berbere. Perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the onions and cook until they start to caramelize, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, berbere spice mix, tumeric, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the red split lentils and stir to coat with the spice mixture.
Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
While the lentils cook, prepare the tadka by heating 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a small saucepan over high heat.
Add 1/4 cup of water to the hot oil and immediately remove from heat. This will create a spiced oil.
To serve, stir the cooked lentil mixture with the spiced oil and sweetened condensed milk.
Garnish with chopped cilantro, if using.
